document.title: Risk Management Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Internal risk management report for the ADD Abyz Digital Detector prepared under EN ISO 14971:2012 and related medical electrical equipment standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports the technical documentation/risk management file by documenting risk analysis, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information collection.
keyPoints: Product Name: Abyz Digital Detector; Model Name: ADD.
ADD is a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, not for mammography.
The risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012.
Thirty hazards were identified and all thirty controlled hazards were evaluated as acceptable.
Overall residual risks are stated to be minimized and acceptable.
No products had been sold yet at the time of the post-production information section.

evidence.claim: ADD is the model name and Abyz Digital Detector is the product name.
evidence.evidenceText:
Product Name: Abyz Digital Detector

Model Name: ADD

evidence.claim: ADD is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: ADD is a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector that can generate Images of any part of the body.

evidence.claim: The risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2012 and IEC 60601-1:2006/AMD1:2013 among other standards.
evidence.evidenceText:
EN ISO 14971:2012

Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

IEC 60601-1:2006/AMD1:2013

Medical electrical equipment Part 1: General requirement for basic safety and essential performance

evidence.claim: ADD is designed for an approximately 5-year expected service life.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity multiplied by probability.
Risk levels 1 to 4 are defined as acceptable and 5 to 25 as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText:
Green Zone

Acceptable Region

Risk Level: 1~4

Red Zone

Unacceptable Region

Risk Level: 5~25

evidence.claim: Thirty hazards were identified and all thirty were initially unacceptable in the risk evaluation table.
After risk control, thirty controlled hazards were acceptable and none were unacceptable.
Completeness of risk control was evaluated as acceptable for all thirty identified risks.
Overall residual risks were considered minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Result: Overall residual risks are minimized, therefore, it is considered to be acceptable

evidence.claim: The conclusion states the overall residual risk is acceptable.
The quality management system is based on EN ISO 13485:2016.
evidence.evidenceText: We have been operating a quality management system. Quality management system based on EN ISO13485: 2016.

evidence.claim: No products had been sold yet at the time of the post-production information section.
evidence.evidenceText: No products have been sold yet. However, we will proceed this clause refer to the ‘Clause 9. Information related to post-production’ in the risk management plan after sales volume.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is ambiguous: the document is an internal manufacturer risk management report, while the source references EU directives and MEDDEV guidance but does not identify an issuing regulatory authority.
Multiple dates appear: JUN/15/2020 is used for preparation/review/approval and revision establishment, while the risk management conclusion has Date: 2020.07.22.
Source text includes OCR or extraction artifacts and typographical issues, including inconsistent spacing, table flattening, and phrases such as 'Severerity' and 'shall e recorded'.
Some tabular evidence is flattened, so totals and acceptability columns are traceable but require review against the original formatted document.
